author: deme1744
content:
<@774033563732541451> I was recently hitting some new issues (with tests-zkevm@v0.5.0, didn't try the lastest yet) and after investigating noticed they are related to EIP7997. 
The issue was that our generated witness had a node extra in the state nodes because the lookup for that factory address triggered an access (so even for the no-op, just checking if deployed or not). Not to hard to fix, basically avoid adding that access in this case.

However, looking into that EIP did make me question if the stateless execution of that fork transition block can ever work currently IF the factory contract actually needs to get deployed. Wouldn't the state root differ always?
